title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL sink accepts malformed pool names
summary: The Oracle MySQL sink did not validate configured connection pool names.
observed: A pool name with spaces could be accepted and then delegated to Oracle MySQL Connector/Python.
expected: Pool names must use a small allow-listed character set.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for pool name validation.
impact: Invalid pool names can produce unclear driver errors and inconsistent local behavior.
delivery_semantics: The fix is startup validation only and does not alter write behavior.
security: Allow-listing operational identifiers keeps configuration surfaces simple and reviewable.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL pool names reject spaces and unsupported characters.
Simple alphanumeric pool names with dots, underscores, colons, or hyphens remain accepted.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL unit test suite.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the validation fix is published.
